audit_items.proof_trace: In `tlsg_ctx_init`, the result of `ldap_str2charray(lt->lt_cacertdir, CERTPATHSEP)` at L198 is stored in `dirs` without any NULL check; the subsequent loop `for (i=0; dirs[i]; i++)` at L200 immediately dereferences `dirs`, causing a null pointer dereference if `ldap_str2charray` returns NULL (e.g., on OOM or if `lt->lt_cacertdir` is NULL while `lo->ldo_tls_cacertdir` is non-NULL). The apparent null-name risk in `tlsg_session_chkhost` (L600-637) is mitigated by the explicit NULL-to-localhost guard in the caller (`tls2.c:1126-1129`); nearly all other dereferences in the file are properly guarded.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An LDAP daemon configured with `TLSCACertificatePath` that encounters memory exhaustion (OOM) during TLS context initialization will reach `ldap_str2charray` returning NULL; the unguarded `dirs[i]` dereference then crashes the process, yielding a denial of service reachable via any LDAP TLS connection attempt that triggers context setup.
